name: weighted close grip chin-up on dip cage
instructions: Stand in front of the dip cage and grab the parallel bars with an underhand grip, hands shoulder-width apart.
Hang from the bars with your arms fully extended, feet off the ground, and body straight.
Engage your back muscles and pull your body up towards the bars, keeping your elbows close to your sides.
Continue pulling until your chin is above the bars, then pause for a moment.
Slowly lower your body back down to the starting position, fully extending your arms.
Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

Stand in front of the dip cage and grab the parallel bars with an underhand grip, hands shoulder-width apart. Hang from the bars with your arms fully extended, feet off the ground, and body straight. Engage your back muscles and pull your body up towards the bars, keeping your elbows close to your sides. Continue pulling until your chin is above the bars, then pause for a moment. Slowly lower your body back down to the starting position, fully extending your arms. Repeat for the desired number of repetitions.

conditionNotes.note: Favor symmetrical loading and supported variations if fatigue changes your posture.
Use pain-free range of motion, slower tempo, and lighter progressions when joints feel irritated.
Weight-bearing resistance exercises are beneficial for bone density when load is appropriate.
Avoid loaded spinal flexion and excessive hinge depth. Brace core and use supported positions.
Maintain consistent breathing throughout. Avoid breath-holding.
Both aerobic and resistance training improve insulin sensitivity. Monitor blood sugar before, during, and after sessions. Have a fast-acting carb available.
Rhythm-based exercises and large-amplitude movements are beneficial. Focus on motor confidence.
Focus on muscle co-contraction and joint stability. Avoid locking out joints passively.
Functional patterns are generally safe. Prioritize pelvic floor awareness and avoid Valsalva.
Start light with higher reps and shorter ROM. Progress load gradually as pain-free capacity increases.
